briefing.headline: Rig up a Squarebill Crankbait — aggressive post-spawn approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Squarebill Crankbait is a strong choice for post-spawn with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Pick a crankbait that goes deeper than the depth you're fishing, reel at a pace where you keep it in control. When it hits a rock, try to get it to crawl up and over and then back down the other side -- looks like a crawfish slowly scooting, acts like something between a normal crankbait 

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, riprap, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Hackney: 'At places where shad spawn a little deeper, use a squarebill crankbait matched to the size of the shad. Change out factory trebles with short-shank EWG style and up-size the hook, but be car
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, riprap, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: Square bills are round-bodied with a steep angle and a square bill — this gives them a wide wobble and the best deflection qualities of any crankbait. Most weedless crankbait you can throw. Good through grass, brush, gnarly rocks, riprap. Wor...

Robertson: Spring crankbait progression -- 1) Prespawn: slow-roll lipless on flats near channel banks in 1-6 feet; 2) Postspawn: big-bodied squarebill on tapering points near spawning flats in under 10 feet; 3) Late postspawn: deep diver following...

Wheeler: Post-spawn offshore on Table Rock, the 3 key elements were brushpiles, isolated cedar trees, and bluff end points/big long main-lake points where schools related to current and hard bottom — mixed species (smallmouth, largemouth, spotted ...

Hackney: 'In the South, shad spawn on hard bottoms, matted vegetation, cypress trees, and boat docks. Old Styrofoam marina docks are the best. On the Tennessee River, the better shad spawn is on shell beds and shallow bars in 5-6 feet of water.'

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal post-spawn bass are transitioning and aggressive. Males guard fry in 2-6ft while females roam 5-12ft feeding heavily. Target both shallow fry-guarders and roaming post-spawn females near points.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
